acara_description: Classify triangles, quadrilaterals and other polygons according to their properties.

options: All 3 sides equal and all angles 60°

answer.correct: All 3 sides equal and all angles 60°

default: 'Equilateral' means all sides equal.
If all sides are equal, all angles must also be equal.
3 equal angles each = 180° ÷ 3 = 60°.

questions.worked_solution: Equilateral triangle: all 3 sides equal, all 3 angles = 60°.

default: 'Scalene' means unequal.
All three sides have different lengths.
All three angles are also different.

questions.worked_solution: Scalene: no sides equal (and no angles equal).

options: Exactly 2 pairs of parallel sides but unequal sides

default: A rhombus is like a diamond shape.
Its defining property is 4 equal sides.
It has parallel opposite sides, but angles are not necessarily 90°.

questions.worked_solution: A rhombus has all 4 sides equal.

questions.stem: How many lines of symmetry does an equilateral triangle have?

default: Draw a line from each vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side.
There are 3 vertices, so there are 3 such lines.
3 lines of symmetry.

questions.worked_solution: An equilateral triangle has 3 lines of symmetry.

questions.stem: A square is a special type of:
options: Both a rectangle and a rhombus
Neither a rectangle nor a rhombus

answer.correct: Both a rectangle and a rhombus

default: A rectangle has 4 right angles and opposite sides equal.
A rhombus has all 4 sides equal.
A square has all 4 right angles AND all 4 sides equal.

questions.worked_solution: A square satisfies the conditions for both a rectangle and a rhombus.

questions.stem: How many lines of symmetry does a kite have?

default: A kite has one pair of adjacent equal sides and one pair.
Draw a line along its main axis (joining the two 'tips').
This is the only line that reflects the kite onto itself.

questions.worked_solution: A kite has exactly 1 line of symmetry.

questions.stem: Which of these is NOT a quadrilateral?

default: A quadrilateral has exactly 4 sides.
Count the sides of each shape.
A pentagon has 5 sides, not 4.

questions.worked_solution: Pentagon (5 sides) is not a quadrilateral.

questions.stem: An isosceles triangle has angles of 70°, 70° and 40°. How many lines of symmetry does it have?

default: An isosceles triangle (not equilateral) has exactly 1 line of symmetry.
The line passes from the apex (40° angle) to the midpoint of the base.
1 line of symmetry.

questions.worked_solution: A non-equilateral isosceles triangle has 1 line of symmetry.

questions.stem: Which quadrilateral has diagonals that bisect each other at right angles?

default: Consider which shapes have perpendicular diagonals.
A rhombus has all sides equal; its diagonals cross at right angles.
A rectangle's diagonals bisect each other but not at right angles.

questions.worked_solution: A rhombus has diagonals that bisect each other at right angles.

default: 'Parallelogram' means parallel sides.
Opposite sides are parallel AND equal in length.
Angles are not necessarily 90°.

questions.worked_solution: A parallelogram has 2 pairs of parallel, equal sides.
